person.name: Ada Lovelace
person.headline: Staff software engineer · distributed systems
person.summary: Builds dependable distributed systems and developer platforms, turning ambiguous operational problems into observable, maintainable products. Leads cross-functional delivery while staying close to architecture, implementation, and production feedback.

experience.summary: Owns the architecture and delivery of a multi-region computation platform used by product and data teams.
highlights: Cut recovery time from hours to minutes by introducing deterministic replay and automated reconciliation.
Led a platform migration across six teams without interrupting customer workloads.
Established service-level objectives and production review practices for critical workflows.

experience.summary: Built internal developer tooling and high-volume data services for financial modelling teams.
highlights: Designed a typed workflow system that replaced error-prone manual release coordination.
Improved batch throughput by 4× while reducing peak infrastructure cost.

projects.name: Notes on dependable computation
projects.summary: A technical publication explaining practical reliability techniques through small, reproducible examples.
highlights: Published reviewed explanations and runnable reference implementations.
Used by engineering teams as onboarding material for distributed-systems work.

projects.name: Workflow Observatory
projects.summary: An open-source diagnostic interface for inspecting long-running workflows and their causal history.
highlights: Designed an accessible timeline that remains useful for traces with thousands of events.

details: Focused on numerical methods and formal reasoning.

items.title: Mentoring
items.text: Volunteer mentor for engineers moving into platform and reliability roles.
